production_note: Rebuilt from scratch in main session after the prior subagent pipeline halted with researcher-hallucination findings. Original halted draft (.claude/state/halted/2026-04-30-mango-researcher-hallucination/) cited 8 hallucinated URLs: doa.go.th/hort/mammuang/* and /frc/mammuang/ (404), arda.or.th/th/2022/07/06/15459/ (404), cabidigitallibrary.org/.../32461 (404), esc.doae.go.th/mango-off-season/ + /mango-soil/ (resolve to ESC homepage, not topic pages), pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8840062 (silver-nanoparticles paper, not Wongs-Aree mango), royalprojectthailand.com/product/mango (403, domain now hosts spam). All 11 sources in this rebuild were HTTP-verified by main session via curl GET with browser User-Agent before draft. Mahachanok-as-Royal-Project-cultivar attribution dropped (no verifiable Royal Project source); replaced with Wikipedia + UF/IFAS Mango Science attribution (developed Chiang Mai, named 1992 by Rama IX, hybrid of Sunset × Nang Klanwan). Specific stats from the hallucinated PMC paper (3M rai, 200 cultivars) were dropped; specific export-market list (CN/JP/KR/SG/MY) softened to 'Japan as a notable export market that requires vapour heat treatment' which is supported by AUJT and DOAE doc 3/2565.

1_thailand_applicability.rationale: Suitability across Thai regions and export-market context are documented in DOA HRI database, DOAE export-quality manual, and the AUJT Thai mango export paper.

2_climate.rationale: Climate requirements and the cool/dry pre-flowering period are covered by Purdue NewCROP and reinforced by DOA HRI's Thai-context guidance.

3_soil.rationale: Thai DOA share PDF (aid=2785) and HRI database both cover soil prep for mango.

4_water.rationale: Water management for mango including the dry-period induction is covered by the Thai DOA cultivation document, DOAE ESC off-season curriculum, and Purdue NewCROP.

5_planting.rationale: Propagation and spacing covered by DOA sources. Mahachanok cultivar history (Sunset × Nang Klanwan, named 1992 by Rama IX) cited from Wikipedia and corroborated by UF/IFAS Mango Science phenology page.

6_care.rationale: Fertilization, pruning, and off-season induction (PBZ/thiourea) referenced from DOA cultivation/quality PDFs and DOAE ESC off-season curriculum. No dosages cited per SAFETY_POLICY.

7_pests_diseases.rationale: Major pests (mango leafhopper, fruit fly, seed weevil) and diseases (anthracnose, powdery mildew) are documented by DOA HRI and Purdue. Mango seed weevil quarantine relevance cited via DOAE news. Note: DOAE seed weevil source identified during research but not added as separate row to keep source table at 11 entries — content is broadly supported by DOA HRI.

8_harvest.rationale: Harvest practice and post-harvest handling for export (vapour heat treatment for Japan) are well documented in DOAE export-quality doc and AUJT Thai mango export paper.

9_economics.rationale: Export-vs-domestic split and risks are documented in DOAE manual and AUJT paper, but no specific cost-of-production figures are cited (Kaset Atlas policy: no profit/yield guarantees).

10_thailand_notes.rationale: Regional notes (north/central/northeast/south) align with DOA Thai-context sources. Mahachanok northern origin from Wikipedia.

11_foreign_knowledge.rationale: International sources discussed with explicit applicability flags for Thailand. AUJT cited as Thai-specific complement.

overall_rationale: All 11 source URLs were main-session HTTP-verified (200 OK with browser User-Agent) before draft. Section 9 rated medium because no specific cost-of-production figures are cited (policy compliance). Mahachanok cultivar history downgraded to medium-confidence Wikipedia citation, but corroborated by UF/IFAS Mango Science. All claims previously dependent on hallucinated sources were either re-sourced or removed.
sources_dropped_vs_halted: doae-esc-mango-off-season-th — original URL was ESC homepage, not a topic page
doae-esc-mango-soil-th — original URL was ESC homepage, not a topic page
royalproject-mango-th — domain now hosts spam, no real Royal Project mango page found
pmc-mango-thailand-production-2022 — PMC8840062 is a silver-nanoparticles paper, NOT Wongs-Aree's mango paper
fao-tropical-fruits-mango-chapter — original a0120e/a0120e04.htm 404 on both .org/3/ and .org/4/ paths
cabi-mangifera-indica-compendium — correct DOI is 34505 not 32461; even with correct DOI cabidigitallibrary returns 403 to scripted GET, fails verify-urls.sh; dropped
claims_softened_or_removed: '3 million rai mango area' — dropped; specific stat depended on hallucinated PMC paper
'export markets: China, Japan, Korea, Singapore, Malaysia' — softened to mention Japan specifically (vapour-heat-treatment market) which is supported by DOAE 3/2565 and AUJT
'Mahachanok = Royal Project cultivar' — replaced with Wikipedia-cited 'developed in Chiang Mai, hybrid of Sunset × Nang Klanwan, named in 1992 by King Bhumibol Adulyadej'
Specific yield figures — not included (policy: no yield/profit guarantees)

inline_verifier_findings_2026_04_30.verifier: main-session-inline (post subagent failure — see PIPELINE_FAILURES.md 09:55 entry)
claims_verified_against_fresh_fetch: Section 5 Mahachanok parentage Sunset × Nang Klanwan, developed in Chiang Mai — VERIFIED via Wikipedia
Section 5 Mahachanok fruit weight 365-410 g, fiber-free, yellowish peel with pinkish-red blush — VERIFIED via UF/IFAS Mango Science phenology
Section 5 cultivar list (น้ำดอกไม้, เขียวเสวย, อกร่อง, มหาชนก, โชคอนันต์, แก้ว) — ALL VERIFIED in DOA HRI mango DB
Section 2/3 cool-dry-pre-flowering and full sun — VERIFIED via Purdue NewCROP and UF/IFAS MG216
Section 6 PBZ + thiourea for off-season induction — VERIFIED via DOAE ESC off-season curriculum (chapters 4 + 5)
Section 8/9 export percentages 96% domestic / 2.3% export — VERIFIED via DOAE doc 3/2565
Section 8 vapour heat treatment — confirmed in DOAE 3/2565 keyword list (การอบไอน้ำ)
claims_softened_during_inline_verify: Section 5 Mahachanok: removed unsupported '1992 by King Bhumibol' royal-naming claim (current Wikipedia article does not contain this fact). Kept parentage + Chiang Mai + UF/IFAS-supported fruit characteristics.
inline_verifier_findings_2026_04_30.safety_policy_compliance: pass — no pesticide dosages (PBZ/thiourea named only as compounds), no profit/yield guarantees (explicit disclaimers in section 9), no medical claims, 2 WarningBoxes in sections 6 and 7
